message:
chore(phase2c): refresh test failure surfaces + new conformance drift artifacts

Captures the current differential-suite drift surfaces after recent
fp-frame work. No code changes here — this is the regenerated parity
ledger picking up new test cases and shifted mismatch classes.

Modified surfaces
- FP-P2D-340 (series_rolling_count_null_hardened) shrinks from 5 to 2
  nullness mismatches, reflecting fewer rolling-count NaN drifts after
  the upstream alignment fix.
- FP-P2D-429 (csv_read_frame_parse_dates_mixed_timezone_strict) flips
  from nullness_critical (actual=Null(NaT)) to value_critical
  (actual=Utf8("2024-01-15T10:30:00Z")). The CSV reader is now
  surfacing the timestamp string instead of dropping it; remaining
  drift is ISO-8601 vs space-separated rendering.
- differential_suite_produces_structured_drift, e2e_suite_runs_full_pipeline,
  grouped_reports_are_partitioned_per_packet, packet_suite_is_green_for_bootstrap_cases:
  pick up new lines for FP-P2D-028 (dataframe_concat axis=1 dtype upcast),
  FP-P2D-426 (csv read true/false vs Bool dtype), FP-P2D-429 (timestamp
  rendering above), keeping existing rows intact.

New surfaces
- FP-P2D-028: five dataframe_concat axis=1 cases (basic_strict,
  order_left_then_unseen_hardened, preserves_existing_nulls_hardened,
  sparse_alignment_strict, utf8_index_strict) — all value_critical
  Float64-vs-Int64 dtype upcasts that concat is producing instead of
  preserving the input integer dtype.
- FP-P2D-426: csv_read_frame_true_false_values_strict — reader emits
  Int64(0/1) where pandas emits Bool(true/false).
- FP-P2D-433: dataframe_to_json_records_ignores_index_and_preserves_column_order_strict
  — JSON encoder emits "a":1.0 where pandas emits "a":1 for integer
  values that were stored as Float64 internally.

Conformance jsonl
- artifacts/conformance/<unknown>/csv_read_frame_parse_dates_mixed_timezone_strict.jsonl:
  same drift class flip as the FP-P2D-429 surface above.
- New artifacts/conformance/<unknown>/ rows for the FP-P2D-028 /
  FP-P2D-426 / FP-P2D-433 cases above.

These are signals from the differential harness, not regressions —
the parity gate continues to track them as part of the
phase2c packets backlog.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

files.patch:
@@ -0,0 +1 @@
+{"suite":"phase2c_packets:FP-P2D-426","packet_id":"FP-P2D-426","case_id":"csv_read_frame_true_false_values_strict","operation":"csv_read_frame","mode":"strict","mismatch_class":"value_critical","mismatch":"[Value/Critical] dataframe.columns.flag.values[0]: value mismatch: actual=Int64(1), expected=Bool(true); [Value/Critical] dataframe.columns.flag.values[1]: value mismatch: actual=Int64(0), expected=Bool(false)","replay_key":"FP-P2D-426/csv_read_frame_true_false_values_strict/strict","trace_id":"FP-P2D-426:csv_read_frame_true_false_values_strict:strict","evidence_records":0}
